title: selago stewartii
intro: Selago stewartii, also known as Stewart's selago, is a plant species belonging to the genus Selago, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Gyrostemonaceae. It is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and fever, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting relaxation. Selago stewartii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
